prompt: In how many ways can 1,000,000 be written as an unordered sum of two primes p + q (p <= q)? Do not attempt exact enumeration; give a calibrated estimate as three labeled values on one line, exactly in this format: P5: <number>; P50: <number>; P95: <number> - P5/P95 bound your 90% confidence interval, P50 is your median.
notes: Truth by direct sieve pairing = 5,402 (verify_answers.py). Differential shape: the naive n/(2 ln^2 n) heuristic WITHOUT the Hardy-Littlewood singular-series correction anchors ~2x low (~2,600) -- exactly the miss a mechanical interval-widening rule contains and a reflective-but-anchored interval may not.
